target.name: cylib
target.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Cylib is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, and Paul Sabarny. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector companies in Climate & Energy, alongside a broader universe of 8 private comparables and 1 public reference point (LNZA). This peer-median approach reflects observable market pricing for early-stage energy technology.

The peer-median method applied to the three identified comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This represents the core triangulation output given the current data availability. Cylib has raised €140M+ to date across its Series A round (€55M) and government grants (€26.1M NRW, €63.4M BMWE), positioning it competitively within this peer cohort.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M. The narrow methodological basis—one applicable approach at present—represents the primary constraint on conviction. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of subsequent fundraising, differentiation relative to the five top private peers identified (including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials), and regulatory tailwinds embedded in the government grant structure. Validation of this estimate would benefit from revenue or technology-stage metrics not present in the current dataset.
